methods.description: Procedures for determining the texture, physico-chemical properties (pH, organic carbon, organic nitrogen), major and trace element composition, extractable iron content, and soil organic matter of sediment/soil samples.

steps.description: The texture of the horizons was determined by the hydrometer method (van Reeuwijk 1992).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine soil texture using the hydrometer method.

steps.description: For physico-chemical analysis, the same samples were taken and analyzed for their pH in a 1:2.5 0.01 M CaCl 2 -suspension.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure pH of soil suspension in 0.01 M CaCl2.

steps.description: For physico-chemical analysis, the same samples were taken and analyzed for organic carbon (C org ) according to the Walkley-Black procedure (Page et al. 1982).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine organic carbon content using the Walkley-Black procedure.

steps.description: For physico-chemical analysis, the same samples were taken and analyzed for organic nitrogen using the Kjeldahl procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine organic nitrogen content using the Kjeldahl procedure.

steps.description: Major (Al, Fe, and Mn) and trace (S, Cr, Cu, Pb, Zn, Co, Ni, Cd, Sr, Hg, V) elements were determined using an X-ray fluorescence (XRF) spectrometer (SpectroXepos-Benchtop-Roentgenfluoreszenz-Spektrometer) equipped with a Rh tube (Chen et al. 2001).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure major and trace element concentrations using XRF spectrometry.
outputs: Concentrations of Al, Fe, Mn, S, Cr, Cu, Pb, Zn, Co, Ni, Cd, Sr, Hg, V

steps.description: Quality control of the analytical procedure was established using three internationally recognized standard reference materials (SRMs) viz., ISE, BCSS-1, and MESS-1. Based on these standards, the accuracy and precision of the analysis were within 1% for major elements and within 5% for trace elements.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quality control for XRF analysis using standard reference materials.
outputs: Accuracy and precision metrics for XRF analysis

steps.description: Oxalate Fe o and dithionite Fe d extractable iron (Holmgren 1967) were measured by inductively coupled plasma spectrometry (ICP).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract and measure oxalate and dithionite extractable iron using ICP.

steps.description: The soil organic matter (SOM) has been calculated as C% * 1.72.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate soil organic matter content from organic carbon content.

methods.method_name: OSL Dating SAR Protocol - Preliminary Tests and Parameter Selection
methods.description: Procedures for performing preliminary tests (preheat, dose recovery, fading) on sediment samples to check suitability for OSL dating using the SAR protocol and to select optimal experimental parameters like preheat temperature.

steps.description: Before starting the OSL measurements with a single aliquot regenerative dose (SAR) protocol, several tests were performed to check the suitability of the material for dating.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess material suitability for OSL dating.

steps.description: The SAR protocol based on Murray & Wintle (2000) was used in this study. The protocol was modified by inserting a hot-bleach step and the cutheat temperature was chosen equivalent to the preheat temperature (Wallinga et al. 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Choose and specify the SAR OSL dating protocol with modifications.
inputs: Knowledge of OSL protocols (Murray & Wintle 2000, Wallinga et al. 2007)

steps.description: Preheat tests, dose recovery tests and fading tests were performed.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct preliminary OSL tests (preheat, dose recovery, fading).
outputs: Raw data from preheat, dose recovery, and fading tests

steps.description: The tests were made for the top-most samples and the bottom-most samples in the profiles. Samples SEE 1-A and SEE 1-F from profile Schulwiese and samples SEE 2-A and SEE 2-C from profile Kartoffelfeld were used for preheat tests. Samples SEE 1-C and SEE 2-A were used for a dose recovery test. The fading tests were done for all samples using fresh aliquots.
steps.searchable_summary: Select and prepare specific samples/aliquots for preliminary OSL tests.
inputs: Sediment samples from profiles Schulwiese and Kartoffelfeld (SEE 1-A, SEE 1-F, SEE 2-A, SEE 2-C, SEE 1-C)

steps.description: For the samples from profile Schulwiese, the D e show a plateau in the temperature range of 160°C to 240°C (SEE 1-A) and 180°C to 240°C (SEE 1-F). For higher preheat temperatures the D e are increasing significantly. The recycling ratios are in the range of acceptance of 1.0 ± 0.1 for preheat temperatures up to 240°C. For higher preheat temperatures the recycling ratios are decreasing. The recuperation is increasing for preheat temperatures higher than 240°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze OSL preheat test data for Schulwiese samples to identify optimal temperature range.
inputs: Preheat test data (De, recycling ratios, recuperation) for SEE 1-A and SEE 1-F

steps.description: Based on these results a preheat temperature of 200°C for all the samples from profile Schulwiese was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Select optimal preheat temperature for Schulwiese samples based on test results.
outputs: Selected preheat temperature for Schulwiese samples (200°C)

steps.description: The behaviour for the samples from profile Kartoffelfeld differs. There is no clear preheat plateau visible for the samples SEE 2-A and SEE 2-C. The D e are slightly increasing with increasing preheat temperature. The recycling ratios are decreasing and scattering for preheat temperatures higher than 220°C. The recuperation is increasing exponentially for preheat temperatures higher than 200°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze OSL preheat test data for Kartoffelfeld samples.
inputs: Preheat test data (De, recycling ratios, recuperation) for SEE 2-A and SEE 2-C
outputs: Analysis of preheat behavior for Kartoffelfeld samples

steps.description: Based on the behaviour of the recycling ratios and recuperation, a preheat temperature of 180°C was used for all samples from the profile Kartoffelfeld.
steps.searchable_summary: Select optimal preheat temperature for Kartoffelfeld samples based on test results.
outputs: Selected preheat temperature for Kartoffelfeld samples (180°C)

steps.description: After determination of the appropriate preheat tempe-rature, dose-recovery tests based on the protocol described by Murray & Wintle (2003) were carried out. This test determines whether an artificially applied dose can be recovered by the SAR protocol. For the profile Schulwiese 15 aliquots from sample SEE 1-C and 10 aliquots from sample SEE 2-A (profile Kartoffelfeld) were bleached in a Dr. Hönle SOL 2 solar simulator for 2 hours. The bleached samples are irradiated in the Risø Reader with a fixed beta-dose which is close to the expected equivalent dose. This dose was measured us-
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct OSL dose recovery test procedure.
outputs: Measured equivalent dose (De) from the applied dose for dose recovery test

steps.description: The fading tests were done for all samples using fresh aliquots.
